Full Stack Development.question: In a typical MERN stack, which component is primarily responsible for persisting structured data with relationships?
Which practice best reduces duplicate API calls when multiple child components need the same user session data?
options: Call fetch in every useEffect without dependencies
Lift shared fetch into a parent or context provider

Full Stack Development.question: What does REST emphasize for resource identification?
Why is separating validation on both client and server important?
options: Client checks UX; server enforces security and data integrity
Server validation is never needed if TypeScript is used
Only mobile apps need server validation

Full Stack Development.question: Which pair best describes a common full-stack deployment unit?
options: API service + static SPA assets behind a reverse proxy

Full Stack Development.question: In Express, what is the primary role of middleware?
options: Run functions in the request-response pipeline (auth, parsing, logging)

Full Stack Development.question: What is a pragmatic reason to version REST APIs (e.g., /v1)?
options: Ship breaking changes without silently breaking existing clients

Full Stack Development.question: Which database concern is most associated with ACID transactions?
options: Relational engines like PostgreSQL for critical writes

Full Stack Development.question: What does JWT statelessness imply for typical API design?
options: Server verifies signature/expiry; session state is mostly in the token claims
Passwords are stored in the token payload in plain text

Full Stack Development.question: Which approach helps avoid N+1 query problems in ORMs?
options: Fetching one row at a time in a loop without includes
Eager loading / joins when fetching related rows

Full Stack Development.question: What is a key benefit of environment-based configuration (e.g., DATABASE_URL)?
options: Deploy the same build to dev/stage/prod with different secrets
Hardcode secrets in the repo for speed

Full Stack Development.question: In React, what problem do keys in lists mainly address?
options: Help reconcile which items changed for efficient updates

Full Stack Development.question: Which layering choice commonly separates routing/controllers from persistence logic?
options: Storing JSX in PostgreSQL blobs only

Full Stack Development.question: CORS protects primarily which scenario?
options: Browser-enforced cross-origin policy for fetched resources

Full Stack Development.question: What is GraphQL introspection commonly used for during development?
options: Discover schema types from a running endpoint

Full Stack Development.question: Which testing layer best targets rendering and user-visible behavior for a SPA?
Why hydrate a SSR React app on the client?
options: Attach event listeners after server HTML arrives for interactivity

Frontend Development.question: What does the cascade refer to in CSS?
options: How conflicting declarations resolve by origin, specificity, and order

Frontend Development.question: Which DOM API is preferable for querying a single element when you manage an explicit id?
Why memoize expensive derived values in React with useMemo?
options: Avoid redoing costly computation on unrelated renders

Frontend Development.question: What problem does semantic HTML primarily help solve?
options: Meaningful structure for accessibility and parsers

Frontend Development.question: Which Flexbox alignment places items along the cross-axis of a container?
What is virtualization in long lists?
options: Render only visible viewport rows for efficiency

Frontend Development.question: What is the closure created by function components captures from outer scope?
options: Values from renders unless dependencies are managed (useEffect/useCallback refs)

Frontend Development.question: Which fetch mode is restricted by browsers for opaque cross-origin responses unless CORS permits?
What does WCAG emphasize for interactive controls?
Why bundle split a large SPA?
options: Reduce initial payload and improve time-to-interactive

Frontend Development.question: What is JSX under the hood in React?
options: A separate browser language interpreted natively

Frontend Development.question: Which debugging practice helps pinpoint unnecessary re-renders?
options: React DevTools Profiler and memo boundaries

Frontend Development.question: What is defer on a classic script tag meant to do?
options: Download in parallel but execute after HTML parsing in order

Frontend Development.question: Why prefer controlled inputs for forms in React?
options: Single source of truth in component state simplifies validation UX

Frontend Development.question: What does responsive design prioritize across breakpoints?
options: Layouts and assets that adapt to viewport sizes

Frontend Development.question: Which caching header pair is commonly used for fingerprinted immutable assets?
options: Expires set to Unix epoch always

Frontend Development.question: What is portals in React used for?
options: Render children into a DOM node outside parent hierarchy

Backend Development.question: What is idempotency in HTTP APIs?
options: Every POST must change random IDs
Repeating safe requests yields the same observable effect

Backend Development.question: Which index trade-off is most accurate?
options: Faster lookups at cost of slower writes and storage
Indexes always reduce read latency infinitely
Indexes are only for full table scans

Backend Development.question: Why use prepared statements / parameter binding?
options: Mitigate SQL injection while allowing query plan caching

Backend Development.question: What role does Redis frequently play beside PostgreSQL?
options: Primary transactional ledger with ACID by default everywhere
Caching, rate limiting, pub/sub ephemeral workloads

Backend Development.question: Race conditions during inventory decrement are commonly handled by:
options: Transactions with row locking or optimistic concurrency tokens

Backend Development.question: What describes a brittle synchronous chain across many microservices?
options: Higher tail latency cascading failures versus async messaging

Backend Development.question: Which status code suits validation errors caused by malformed client input?
What is connection pooling?
options: Reuse DB connections instead of reopening each request

Backend Development.question: Why propagate correlation IDs?
options: Trace requests through services in logs/traces

Backend Development.question: What distinguishes symmetric vs asymmetric crypto for securing transport?
options: TLS handshakes use asymmetric to agree symmetric session keys broadly
Only asymmetric for bulk data encryption at wire speed unchanged

Backend Development.question: What pattern isolates integrations with flaky third-parties?
options: Circuit breaker with timeouts and backoff

Backend Development.question: Why pagination for large datasets?
options: Bound latency and memory for clients and databases
Guarantee O(1) for every analytic query automatically

Backend Development.question: What is eventual consistency?
options: Replicas converge over time rather than instantaneous strong consistency everywhere

Backend Development.question: Which hashing approach is suited for passwords?
What motivates using message queues?
options: Decouple producers/consumers, smooth spikes, improve reliability patterns

Backend Development.question: Schema migrations primarily help:
options: Evolve relational structure with versioned repeatable steps

Data Science & Analytics.question: Missing values are often addressed by imputation vs dropping when:
options: Dropping removes too much data or biases samples

Data Science & Analytics.question: What does Pearson correlation measure?
options: Linear association between two quantitative variables (−1 to 1)

Data Science & Analytics.question: Overfitting manifests as:
options: Poor training and great test simultaneously
Great training scores but worse generalization to unseen data
Always larger batch sizes fixing everything

Data Science & Analytics.question: Precision answers which question?
options: Of actual positives, how many did we find? (Recall)
Of predicted positives, how many are truly positive?

Data Science & Analytics.question: Feature scaling affects many distance-based algos because:
options: Large magnitude features would otherwise dominate gradients/distances

Data Science & Analytics.question: Why stratify splits for classification?
options: Guarantee every split has outliers removed
Preserve class proportions reduce sampling variance across folds/splits

Data Science & Analytics.question: What is regularization broadly trying to penalize?
options: Unduly complex parameter settings that memorize noise

Data Science & Analytics.question: A confusion matrix for binary classification summarizes:
EDA before modelling helps mainly to:
options: Spot anomalies, distributions, leakage risks, informed transforms

Data Science & Analytics.question: Leakage arises when:
options: Training uses information unavailable at prediction time unknowingly
You log transform skewed positives only

Data Science & Analytics.question: Cross-validation contrasts with a single holdout by:
options: Always guaranteeing unbiased production metrics miraculously
Averaging performance across folds to reduce variance in estimates

Data Science & Analytics.question: Bagging ensembles reduce variance mainly by:
options: Bootstrapped samples aggregated models decorrelating mistakes
Adding many linear layers sequentially only

Data Science & Analytics.question: Why visualize residuals in regression?
options: Reveal heteroskedasticity, non-linearity missed by linearity assumptions

Data Science & Analytics.question: Normalization vs standardization:
options: Only for categorical encodings interchangeably mistaken
Min-max bounded range vs zero-mean unit-variance Gaussian scaling heuristic

Data Science & Analytics.question: One-hot encoding categorical variables:
options: Guarantees tree models better without high cardinality safeguards
Eliminates bogus ordinal magnitude between unordered categories

Data Science & Analytics.question: Bayes theorem swaps conditioning to relate:
options: Posterior likelihood with prior predictive evidence framing

DevOps Engineering.question: What describes infrastructure as code (IaC)?
options: Declaratively version infrastructure definitions applied by tooling

DevOps Engineering.question: Kubernetes Deployments rolling updates aim for:
Why multi-stage Dockerfile builds?
options: Separate compile vs runtime artifacts to shrink secure images
Bundle dev dependencies into prod always

DevOps Engineering.question: CI pipelines primarily enforce:
options: Automated test/build artifact validation on SCM events

DevOps Engineering.question: Liveness vs readiness probes:
options: Restart unhealthy vs temporarily remove routing until dependencies ready

DevOps Engineering.question: Canary releases reduce risk by:
options: Routing gradual traffic shifts with monitoring thresholds

DevOps Engineering.question: Secrets in Git:
options: Should use secret managers injection not committed plaintext blobs
Should use base64 only as encryption

DevOps Engineering.question: Horizontal pod autoscaling reacts to:
options: Only manual kubectl scale forever only
Metrics like CPU/custom signals crossing thresholds schedules more replicas when policies allow state rules

DevOps Engineering.question: Blue/green strategy highlights:
options: Two identical environments swapping traffic pointers quickly

DevOps Engineering.question: SLI drives SLO/SLA by measuring:
options: Specific quantifiable indicators of service quality

DevOps Engineering.question: Immutable infra mindset prefers:
options: Replace instances/containers versus mutating drifting state SSH hotfixes blindly
Only apt upgrade live prod ad hoc undocumented

DevOps Engineering.question: What is systemd unit commonly used for on Linux VMs?
options: Manage daemon lifecycle restart policies dependencies

DevOps Engineering.question: Why backoff with jitter retries?
options: Avoid thundering herd synchronized retries harming recovering systems

DevOps Engineering.question: Container image tagging best practice:
options: Immutable digest or semantic version tags pinned in manifests not drifting latest alone without controls

DevOps Engineering.question: Feature flags progressively enable code by:
options: Runtime toggling decoupling deploy from activation risk limits
